name: Structure of Arrays (SoA)
def: Storing each field in its own contiguous array instead of an array of whole structs (AoS).
problem: Maximise cache utilisation and enable SIMD when code processes one field across many items.
mechanism: Transpose AoS {x,y,z}[] into SoA {x[], y[], z[]}; iterating a single field touches only contiguous, vectorisable memory.
limits: Per-entity access is scattered across arrays
Less intuitive; verbose to mutate one entity
Keeping arrays in sync is error-prone
failures: Cache thrash when whole-entity access dominates
myths: "SoA is always faster" — AoS wins when you touch all fields of one item
